THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED, ALBEIT WITHOUT A SEPARATE STATEMENT. WHEN DEFENDANTS LATER FILED THE SEPARATE STATEMENT, PLAINTIFF ASKED THAT THE MOTION BE RE-NOTICED. PLAINTIFF NOW ARGUES THAT THE RE-NOTICED MOTION IS UNTIMELY. THE COURT FINDS THIS ARGUMENT UNPERSUASIVE AS, AFTER ALL, IT WAS PLAINTIFF THAT ASKED THAT THE MOTION BE RE-NOTICED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE VARIOUS DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND GRANTS THE MOTION AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 9.1, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 12.4; SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 2, 5, 6, 7, 13, 15, 22, 24, 27, 28, RFPD NOS. 1-4, 7-10. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 9.2, 11.1, SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NOS. 1, 4, 26 AND 31. SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO BE PROVIDED BY NOVEMBER 1, 2019. BECAUSE OF DEFENDANTS' ERROR IN NOT FILING A TIMELY SEPARATE STATEMENT, THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Defendants Ajloun Enterprises Llc And Muath Zghoul'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Written Discovery Responses And Documents
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: OCTOBER 10, 2019 LINE 2,
NO APPEARANCE:
Defendants Ajloun Enterprises Llc And Muath Zghoul'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Written Discovery Responses And Documents IS granted in part. The Court finds that the motion was timely filed, albeit without a Separate Statement. When Defendants later filed the Separate Statement, Plaintiff asked that the motion be re-noticed. Plaintiff now argues that the re-noticed motion is untimely. The Court finds this argument unpersuasive as, after all, it was Plaintiff that asked that the motion be re-noticed. The Court has reviewed the various discovery requests and grants the motion as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 9.1, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 12.4; Special Interrogatory Nos. 2, 5, 6, 7, 13, 15, 22, 24, 27, 28, RFPD Nos. 1-4, 7-10. The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory No. 9.2, 11.1, Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 4, 26 and 31. Supplemental responses to be provided by November 1, 2019. Because of Defendants' error in not filing a timely Separate Statement, the request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to prepare Order. Judge: David McDonald; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany (jpt/302).
